accepted.generator_rule: Detect the fixed 10x10 gray separator grid and color the first, middle, and last cells blue, red, and green.

proof.complete_generator_domain: 17 valid widths x 8 valid heights = 136/136 in both ORT modes; raw-equal to pinned baseline on all 136
proof.runtime_shapes: 65/65 node outputs match inferred shapes in both ORT modes

rejected.generator_rule: Join each same-colored endpoint pair by its length-3..7 diagonal in either diagonal direction.
reasons: The incumbent is shape-cloaked: 76 CenterCropPad nodes carry false compact declarations.
The pinned member is 266/266 with ORT_DISABLE_ALL but default ORT has 266/266 runtime errors.
Truthful Boolean exposure costs 437668; harvested truthful/static alternatives begin at cost 497, above 374.

rejected.generator_rule: Join the two endpoints of each unique-colored horizontal or vertical stick; the random generator bug only emits horizontal sticks, while known fixtures include both.
reasons: The incumbent declares output [1,10,1,1] while ORT returns [1,10,30,30], and similarly hides full input/index tensors behind CenterCropPad.
The cheapest archived sub-baseline model costs 340 but is only 221/265 known-correct and therefore cannot use the private true-rule exception.
All known-complete harvested alternatives profile at cost at least 366 and retain shape cloaks; the earlier truthful family starts at 393.

rejected.generator_rule: Each color marks two opposite corners; fill the complete axis-aligned rectangle between them.
reasons: The incumbent is a prohibited 47-input giant Einsum, so it is not an acceptable reconstruction parent.
There is no intermediate memory to shave; all cost is 312 parameters.
Eight prior factor deletions at costs 282/287 score 0/267 on seven variants and 14/267 on one.

rejected.generator_rule: Extract the separate diagonally connected 3x3 sprite and magnify it inside the red square frame.
reasons: The incumbent has eight known declared/runtime shape contradictions and a 22-input terminal Einsum.
The compact family reproducibly segfaults ORT on legal generator inputs, so it cannot satisfy runtime-error zero.
The archive scan covered 35 models; the cheaper costs 236/291/291 are known-wrong.

rejected.generator_rule: Recover the compact 2x2/2x3/3x2/3x3 color table from the rectangular quilt.
reasons: The incumbent GroupNormalization output is declared [1,1,1,1] but executes as [1,10,30,30]; the model is shape-cloaked.
Its prior strict fresh audit was 4996/5000, so it is not itself a 100% true-rule equivalence certificate.
The archive scan covered 29 models; the only cheaper model costs 314 and is known-wrong.

rejected.generator_rule: Erase the four colored inner-corner markers and place them at the four diagonally opposite outer corners.
reasons: The coordinate carrier hides four to eight 16-way tensors behind false [1,1] CenterCropPad declarations.
The archive scan covered 26 models; the only cheaper cost-294 candidate is 0/266 known-correct.
The complete-known alternate only ties cost 302 and remains shape-cloaked.

rejected.generator_rule: Repeat the top-row color sequence twice as constant-color rows below the gray separator.
reasons: The truthful incumbent is already at 320 memory plus 51 parameters.
Thirty historical models were reprofiled; all eleven cost-370 scale-sharing variants fail known gold.
Trimming the zero Conv kernel column reaches 361 only with illegal negative padding; standard alternatives cost 484 or 511.
